title: Matrix Multiplication, Simplified
concept: Matrix multiplication multiplies a data matrix by a weight vector to compute predictions for an entire dataset in one single step.

exercises.prompt: Recap: what mathematical operation multiplies matching elements of two vectors and adds them up?
exercises.explain: Right — the dot product. But how do we apply this to a whole matrix of examples at once?

cards.title: Batch processing
cards.body: If you have 1,000 houses, you could run 1,000 separate dot products to guess their prices. Or, you could do it in a single batch. That's the power of matrix multiplication.

cards.title: Matrix-vector multiplication
cards.body: Matrix multiplication applies a weight vector to a data matrix by taking the dot product of the weight vector with each row (example) in the matrix, returning a list of predictions.

cards.title: An assembly line
cards.body: Think of the matrix as a conveyor belt of items, and the weights vector as a stamp. As each item (row) passes, the stamp (dot product) prints a score (prediction) on it.

cards.title: Predicting 2 houses at once
cards.body: A matrix has two rows (houses): [2000, 3] and [1500, 2]. The weight vector is [100, 10000]. The result is a new vector of two prices: [230000, 170000].

cards.title: Not element-by-element
cards.body: Matrix multiplication isn't just multiplying matching grid spots. It's a structured combination of row-by-column dot products. The shapes must match: columns in matrix = items in weight vector!

exercises.prompt: What does matrix-vector multiplication do in machine learning?

options.text: Computes predictions for an entire dataset in one mathematical step
Adds a column to the dataset
Multiplies every number by a constant

exercises.explain: It applies the weights to every row in the matrix in a single batch operation.

exercises.prompt: True or false: in matrix-vector multiplication, the number of columns in the matrix must equal the number of elements in the vector.
exercises.explain: True — because each multiplication is a dot product, the row length (columns) must match the weight vector length.

exercises.sentence: Matrix multiplication takes the ___ product of the weights vector with each row of the data matrix.
exercises.explain: It performs a dot product for each row in the matrix against the weight vector.

exercises.prompt: You multiply a 5x3 data matrix by a 3x1 weight vector. What shape is the output?
exercises.explain: You get 5 outputs (one for each row), represented as a 5x1 vector.

exercises.prompt: You have a matrix of 3 users with 2 features [age, income]: [[20, 50], [40, 100], [30, 80]]. The weight vector is [2, 0.5]. What are the predictions for the 3 users?
exercises.explain: Brilliant! Row 1: (20×2)+(50×0.5)=65. Row 2: (40×2)+(100×0.5)=130. Row 3: (30×2)+(80×0.5)=100. Next: what is the space where these vectors live?
